do_not_merge.reason: Same street (Cross Bay Boulevard) and borough (Queens) but different limits: 'north of 165 Av' vs 'at Liberty Avenue and 109 Avenue'. Per corridor merge rule: same street with differing limits never merges (Decision Test 1, strong identity field 'limits').

corridor_cross-bay-blvd-2015 has specific limits 'north of 165 Av'; corridor_cross-bay-blvd-sbs-spring2015 lacks explicit limits and represents a broader study corridor. Per per-kind rule: a broad study corridor and a specific segment are distinct; relate with subsegment_of, never merge.
Same street and borough but different limits: 'north of 165 Av' vs 'between Rockaway Boulevard and 165 Avenue'. Distinct segments; limits differ (Decision Test 1).

corridor_cross-bay-blvd-liberty-109av-2015 has specific limits 'at Liberty Avenue and 109 Avenue'; corridor_cross-bay-blvd-sbs-spring2015 is a broad study corridor without limits. Per per-kind rule: broad and specific are distinct.
Different limits: 'at Liberty Avenue and 109 Avenue' vs 'between Rockaway Boulevard and 165 Avenue'. Limits contradict (Decision Test 1).
corridor_cross-bay-boulevard-rockaway-to-165 has specific limits; corridor_cross-bay-blvd-sbs-spring2015 is a broad study corridor. Distinct per per-kind rule.

weak_aliases.reason: Common street key useful for search, not merge authority.
Common street key useful for search, not merge authority.
Multiple aliases derived from source labels; all refer to the same broad corridor but are not authoritative for merging with specific segments.
Common street key and variant description useful for search.

suggestion.rationale: All four records are on Cross Bay Boulevard in Queens but have distinct limits (or broad scope in the case of sbs-spring2015). The corridor merge rule requires street + borough + limits; differing limits force do_not_merge. The broad study corridor is distinct from each specific segment per the per-kind rule. No ambiguous pairs remain.
